en.الكتاب: The book — a written compilation of hadith, central to the question of written vs. oral transmission
en.يجوز: Is it permissible — jurisprudential term indicating a question of legal permissibility

es.الكتاب: El libro — compilación escrita de hadices

fa.اروه: آن را روایت کن — نقل رسمی حدیث

fr.الكتاب: Le livre — compilation écrite de hadiths

ur.اروه: اسے روایت کرو — حدیث کی رسمی نقل
ur.يجوز: جائز ہے — فقہی اصطلاح، جواز کا سوال

zh.اروه: 传述它——正式的圣训传述

seo_questions.bn: লেখকের মৌখিক অনুমতি ছাড়া কিতাব থেকে হাদিস বর্ণনা করা কি জায়েজ?
seo_questions.de: Ist es im Islam erlaubt, Hadithe aus einem Buch ohne mündliche Genehmigung des Autors zu überliefern?
seo_questions.en: Is it permissible in Islam to narrate hadith from a book without explicit oral permission from its author?
seo_questions.es: ¿Es permisible en el Islam narrar hadices de un libro sin el permiso oral del autor?
seo_questions.fa: آیا روایت حدیث از کتاب بدون اجازه شفاهی مؤلف جایز است؟
seo_questions.fr: Est-il permis en Islam de rapporter un hadith d'un livre sans la permission orale de l'auteur ?
seo_questions.id: Bolehkah meriwayatkan hadis dari kitab tanpa izin lisan penulisnya?
seo_questions.ru: Допустимо ли в Исламе передавать хадисы из книги без устного разрешения автора?
seo_questions.tr: Yazarın sözlü izni olmadan bir kitaptan hadis rivayet etmek caiz midir?
seo_questions.ur: کیا مصنف کی زبانی اجازت کے بغیر کتاب سے حدیث روایت کرنا جائز ہے؟
seo_questions.zh: 在伊斯兰教中，未经作者口头许可从书中传述圣训是否被允许？

similar_content_hints.description: Hadiths from the Imams about the permissibility of transmitting knowledge through written records rather than solely through oral chains, including narrations about the importance of writing down hadith and preserving books.
Narrations about the etiquette and methodology of hadith transmission (riwayah), including the conditions under which one may narrate from another person and the various modes of receiving hadith.

summaries.bn: আহমাদ ইবনে উমার আল-হাল্লাল ইমাম রিদা (আ.)-কে জিজ্ঞাসা করেছিলেন যে লেখকের সুস্পষ্ট মৌখিক অনুমতি ছাড়া কোনো কিতাব থেকে হাদিস বর্ণনা করা জায়েজ কিনা। ইমাম রায় দিলেন যে যদি বর্ণনাকারী নিশ্চিত হন যে কিতাবটি সেই ব্যক্তির, তাহলে বর্ণনা করা যায়। এই হাদিসটি হাদিস শাস্ত্রে লিখিত সংক্রমণের বৈধতা সম্পর্কিত।
summaries.de: Ahmad ibn Umar al-Hallal fragte Imam al-Ridha (F), ob es erlaubt sei, Hadithe aus einem geschriebenen Buch zu überliefern, ohne die ausdrückliche mündliche Erlaubnis des Autors. Der Imam entschied, dass dies zulässig ist, wenn der Überlieferer sicher ist, dass das Buch der betreffenden Person gehört. Dieser Hadith behandelt eine Schlüsselfrage der Hadith-Methodologie über die Gültigkeit schriftlicher Überlieferung.
summaries.en: Ahmad ibn Umar al-Hallal asked Imam al-Ridha (AS) about the permissibility of narrating from a written book when the author did not explicitly grant permission to transmit. The Imam ruled that if the narrator is certain the book belongs to that person, he may report from it. This hadith addresses a key question in hadith methodology (usul al-hadith) about the validity of written transmission (wijadah) versus oral authorization (ijazah), reflecting the early scholarly debates about how religious knowledge could be legitimately transmitted.
summaries.es: Ahmad ibn Umar al-Hallal preguntó al Imam al-Ridha (P) sobre la permisibilidad de narrar hadices de un libro escrito sin el permiso oral explícito de su autor. El Imam dictaminó que si el narrador está seguro de que el libro pertenece a esa persona, puede narrar de él. Este hadiz aborda una cuestión clave en la metodología del hadiz sobre la validez de la transmisión escrita.
summaries.fa: احمد بن عمر حلال از امام رضا علیه السلام درباره جواز روایت از کتابی پرسید که صاحب آن صراحتاً اجازه نقل نداده است. امام فرمودند اگر یقین داشته باشد کتاب متعلق به آن شخص است، روایت از آن جایز است. این حدیث به مسأله‌ای مهم در علم الحدیث یعنی اعتبار وجاده (یافتن کتاب) در مقابل اجازه شفاهی می‌پردازد.
summaries.fr: Ahmad ibn Umar al-Hallal a demandé à l'Imam al-Ridha (P) s'il était permis de rapporter un hadith à partir d'un livre écrit sans l'autorisation orale explicite de son auteur. L'Imam a statué que si le narrateur est certain que le livre appartient à cette personne, il peut en rapporter. Ce hadith traite d'une question clé de la méthodologie du hadith sur la validité de la transmission écrite.
summaries.id: Ahmad bin Umar al-Hallal bertanya kepada Imam al-Ridha (a.s.) tentang hukum meriwayatkan hadis dari sebuah kitab tanpa izin lisan penulisnya. Imam memutuskan bahwa jika perawi yakin kitab itu milik orang tersebut, maka boleh meriwayatkan darinya. Hadis ini membahas validitas transmisi tertulis dalam ilmu hadis.
summaries.ru: Ахмад ибн Умар аль-Халлаль спросил Имама ар-Рида (мир ему), допустимо ли передавать хадисы из письменной книги без прямого устного разрешения автора. Имам постановил, что если передатчик уверен, что книга принадлежит данному лицу, он может передавать от него. Этот хадис затрагивает ключевой вопрос методологии хадиса о допустимости письменной передачи.
summaries.tr: Ahmed b. Ömer el-Hallal, İmam Rıza'ya (a.s.) yazarın açık izni olmaksızın bir kitaptan hadis rivayet etmenin caiz olup olmadığını sormuştur. İmam, kitabın o kişiye ait olduğu bilindiği takdirde bunun caiz olduğuna hükmetmiştir. Bu hadis, hadis ilminde yazılı aktarımın (vicade) meşruiyetine dairdir.
summaries.ur: احمد بن عمر حلال نے امام رضا علیہ السلام سے پوچھا کہ اگر کوئی ساتھی کتاب دے لیکن صراحتاً روایت کی اجازت نہ دے تو کیا اس سے روایت کرنا جائز ہے۔ امام نے فرمایا کہ اگر یقین ہو کہ کتاب اسی شخص کی ہے تو روایت کرنا جائز ہے۔ یہ حدیث تحریری نقل حدیث کی شرعی حیثیت کے بارے میں ہے۔
summaries.zh: 艾哈迈德·本·欧麦尔·哈拉勒询问伊玛目里达（愿主赐他平安），在作者没有明确口头授权的情况下，从书面著作中传述圣训是否允许。伊玛目裁定，如果传述者确定该书属于那个人，则可以从他传述。这段圣训涉及圣训学中关于书面传述有效性的关键问题。

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (179/ 1)
Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: ضعيف - Sahih al-Kafi
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) الرَّجُلُ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا يُعْطِينِي الْكِتَابَ وَ لَا يَقُولُ ارْوِهِ عَنِّي يَجُوزُ لِي أَنْ أَرْوِيَهُ عَنْهُ قَالَ فَقَالَ إِذَا عَلِمْتَ أَنَّ الْكِتَابَ لَهُ فَارْوِهِ عَنْهُ .

en.hubeali: From him, by his chain, from Ahmad Bin Umar Al Hallal who said,

‘I said to Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup>, ‘The man from our companions gave me the book and did not say to me, ‘Report it from me’. Is it allowed for me that I report it from him?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘When you know that the book is his, so report if from him’.
en.sarwar: 6. It is narrated from him from Ahmad ibn ‘Umar al-Hallal who has said the following. “I said to (Imam) abu al-Hassan al-Rida (a.s.), ‘One of our people gives me a book but he does not say whether I can narrate any thing from it or not. Can I narrate from it?’” He has said that the Imam (a.s.) said, “Yes, you can narrate if you would that the book is his.”

translations.bn: আলী ইবনে ইবরাহীম, তাঁর পিতা থেকে, এবং আহমাদ ইবনে মুহাম্মাদ ইবনে খালিদ থেকে, আন-নাওফালী থেকে, আস-সাকুনী থেকে, আবু আবদিল্লাহ আলাইহিস সালাম থেকে বর্ণনা করেছেন যে তিনি বলেছেন: আমীরুল মু'মিনীন আলাইহিস সালাম বলেছেন:
translations.de: Ali ibn Ibrahim, von seinem Vater; und von Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid, von al-Nawfali, von al-Sakuni, von Abu Abdillah (Friede sei mit ihm), der sagte: Amir al-Mu'minin (Friede sei mit ihm) sagte:
translations.en: Ali ibn Ibrahim, from his father, and from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid, from al-Nawfali, from al-Sakuni, from Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) who said: Amir al-Mu'minin (peace be upon him) said:
translations.es: Ali ibn Ibrahim, de su padre; y de Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid, de al-Nawfali, de al-Sakuni, de Abu Abdillah (la paz sea con él) quien dijo: Amir al-Mu'minin (la paz sea con él) dijo:
translations.fa: علی بن ابراهیم از پدرش، و از احمد بن محمد بن خالد، از نوفلی، از سکونی، از ابوعبدالله علیه السلام روایت کرده که فرمود: امیرالمؤمنین علیه السلام فرمود:
translations.fr: Ali ibn Ibrahim, de son père ; et d'Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid, d'al-Nawfali, d'al-Sakuni, d'Abu Abdillah (que la paix soit sur lui) qui a dit : Amir al-Mu'minin (que la paix soit sur lui) a dit :
translations.id: Ali bin Ibrahim, dari ayahnya, dan dari Ahmad bin Muhammad bin Khalid, dari al-Nawfali, dari al-Sakuni, dari Abu Abdillah alaihis salam yang bersabda: Amirul Mukminin alaihis salam bersabda:
translations.ru: Али ибн Ибрахим, от своего отца; и от Ахмада ибн Мухаммада ибн Халида, от ан-Науфали, от ас-Сакуни, от Абу Абдиллаха (мир ему), который сказал: Амир аль-Муминин (мир ему) сказал:
translations.tr: Ali b. İbrahim, babasından; ve Ahmed b. Muhammed b. Halid, en-Nevfeli'den, es-Sekuni'den, o da Ebu Abdullah (a.s.)'dan rivayet etmiştir ki: Müminlerin Emiri (a.s.) buyurdu:
translations.ur: علی بن ابراہیم نے اپنے والد سے، اور احمد بن محمد بن خالد سے، انہوں نے نوفلی سے، انہوں نے سکونی سے، انہوں نے ابو عبد اللہ علیہ السلام سے روایت کی کہ امیر المؤمنین علیہ السلام نے فرمایا:
translations.zh: 阿里·本·易卜拉欣，从他的父亲传述；以及从艾哈迈德·本·穆罕默德·本·哈立德，从努法利，从萨库尼，从阿布·阿卜杜拉（愿主赐他平安）传述，他说：信士的长官（愿主赐他平安）说：

translations.bn: যখন তোমরা কোনো হাদীস বর্ণনা কর, তখন তা যে তোমাদের কাছে বর্ণনা করেছে তার সাথে সম্পৃক্ত কর। যদি তা সত্য হয় তবে তোমাদের জন্য, আর যদি মিথ্যা হয় তবে তার উপর।
translations.de: Wenn ihr einen Hadith berichtet, dann schreibt ihn dem zu, der ihn euch berichtet hat. Denn wenn er wahr war, dann ist es zu eurem Vorteil; und wenn er eine Lüge war, dann liegt es auf ihm.
translations.en: Whenever you narrate a hadith, attribute it to the one who narrated it to you. For if it was true, then it is for you, and if it was a lie, then it is upon him.
translations.es: Cuando narréis un hadiz, atribuidlo a quien os lo narró. Pues si era verdad, será a vuestro favor; y si era mentira, será sobre él.
translations.fa: هنگامی که حدیثی را نقل می‌کنید، آن را به کسی نسبت دهید که برایتان روایت کرده است. پس اگر راست باشد به نفع شماست و اگر دروغ باشد بر عهده اوست.
translations.fr: Lorsque vous rapportez un hadith, attribuez-le à celui qui vous l'a rapporté. Car s'il était vrai, c'est à votre avantage ; et s'il était mensonger, c'est sur lui.
translations.id: Apabila kalian meriwayatkan sebuah hadits, maka sandarkan kepada orang yang meriwayatkannya kepada kalian. Jika hadits itu benar, maka pahalanya untuk kalian, dan jika dusta, maka dosanya atas orang itu.
translations.ru: Когда вы передаёте хадис, возводите его к тому, кто передал его вам. Если он был правдой, то это для вас; а если он был ложью, то это на нём.
translations.tr: Bir hadis rivayet ettiğinizde, onu size rivayet edene nispet edin. Eğer doğru ise sizin lehinizedir, eğer yalan ise onun üzerinedir.
translations.ur: جب تم کوئی حدیث بیان کرو تو اسے اس شخص سے منسوب کرو جس نے تمہیں بتائی ہو۔ پس اگر وہ سچ ہو تو تمہارے لیے ہے، اور اگر جھوٹ ہو تو اس پر ہے۔
translations.zh: 当你们传述一段圣训时，要将其归于向你们传述之人。如果它是真实的，那么功劳归于你们；如果它是谎言，那么罪过归于他。

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ وَعَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنِ النَّوْفَلِيِّ عَنِ السَّكُونِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ قَالَ قَالَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: إِذَا حَدَّثْتُمْ بِحَدِيثٍ فَأَسْنِدُوهُ إِلَى الَّذِي حَدَّثَكُمْ فَإِنْ كَانَ حَقًّا فَلَكُمْ وَإِنْ كَانَ كَذِبًا فَعَلَيْهِ

narrators.known_identity: Ali ibn Ibrahim al-Qummi (d. ~329 AH), prominent Shia hadith scholar and mufassir

narrators.ambiguity_note: Refers to Ibrahim ibn Hashim al-Qummi, father of Ali ibn Ibrahim. Identification based on preceding narrator.
narrators.known_identity: Ibrahim ibn Hashim al-Qummi
Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid al-Barqi (d. 274 AH), author of al-Mahasin
al-Husayn ibn Yazid al-Nawfali, a known transmitter in Shia hadith literature
Isma'il ibn Abi Ziyad al-Sakuni, a narrator of hadith known in both Sunni and Shia sources
Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (AS), the sixth Imam (d. 148 AH)
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (AS), the first Imam and fourth caliph (d. 40 AH)

key_phrases.phrase_en: Attribute it to the one who narrated it to you

bn.أسندوه: সনদ প্রদান কর, উৎসের সাথে সম্পৃক্ত কর — হাদীস বিজ্ঞানের মৌলিক নীতি

de.أسندوه: Schreibt es zu, verbindet es mit seiner Überlieferungskette (Isnad)

en.أسندوه: Attribute it, link it to its chain of narration (isnad). The term 'isnad' is the foundation of hadith authentication methodology.
en.حديث: Hadith, a reported saying or action. In hadith sciences, this specifically refers to a narration with a chain of transmission.

es.أسندوه: Atribuidlo, vinculadlo a su cadena de narración (isnad)

fa.أسندوه: اسناد دهید، به سلسله سند متصل کنید — اصل بنیادین علم حدیث

fr.أسندوه: Attribuez-le, rattachez-le à sa chaîne de transmission (isnad)

id.أسندوه: Sandarkan, hubungkan ke sanadnya — prinsip dasar ilmu hadits

ru.أسندوه: Возведите его, свяжите с цепочкой передатчиков (иснад)

tr.أسندوه: İsnat edin, kaynağına bağlayın — hadis ilimlerinin temel prensibi
tr.حديث: Hadis, rivayet edilen söz veya fiil

ur.أسندوه: اسناد دو، سند سے جوڑو — حدیث کی تصدیق کا بنیادی اصول
ur.حديث: حدیث، روایت — کسی قول یا فعل کی نقل

zh.أسندوه: 将其归于出处，与传述链（伊斯纳德）相连——圣训学的基本原则

seo_questions.bn: ইমাম আলী হাদীসের উৎস উল্লেখ সম্পর্কে কী বলেছেন?
seo_questions.de: Was sagte Imam Ali über die Zuschreibung von Hadithen an ihre Quelle?
seo_questions.en: What did Imam Ali say about attributing hadith to its source?
seo_questions.es: ¿Qué dijo el Imam Ali sobre atribuir los hadices a su fuente?
seo_questions.fa: حضرت علی علیه السلام درباره نسبت دادن حدیث به راوی چه فرمودند؟
seo_questions.fr: Qu'a dit l'Imam Ali sur l'attribution des hadiths à leur source ?
seo_questions.id: Apa yang dikatakan Imam Ali tentang menyandarkan hadits kepada sumbernya?
seo_questions.ru: Что сказал Имам Али о возведении хадисов к их источнику?
seo_questions.tr: Hz. Ali hadislerin kaynağına nispet edilmesi hakkında ne buyurmuştur?
seo_questions.ur: حضرت علی علیہ السلام نے حدیث کو اس کے راوی سے منسوب کرنے کے بارے میں کیا فرمایا؟
seo_questions.zh: 伊玛目阿里关于圣训溯源说了什么？

similar_content_hints.description: Hadiths warning against narrating fabricated reports without verification, such as 'It is sufficient as a lie for a person to narrate everything he hears' attributed to the Prophet (SAWW).
Other narrations in al-Kafi's chapter on hadith transmission and writing that emphasize the preservation of isnad and careful attribution of narrations.
Narrations emphasizing truthfulness and the gravity of lying, particularly in the context of attributing statements to the Prophet or the Imams.

summaries.bn: ইমাম আলী (আ.) নির্দেশ দিয়েছেন যে হাদীস বর্ণনা করার সময় তার উৎস উল্লেখ করতে হবে। হাদীস সত্য হলে বর্ণনাকারী পুরস্কৃত হবে, মিথ্যা হলে মূল বর্ণনাকারী দায়ী হবে।
summaries.de: Imam Ali (Friede sei mit ihm) wies an, dass man bei der Weitergabe eines Hadith die Quelle angeben muss. Ist der Hadith wahr, erhält der Überlieferer den Lohn; ist er falsch, trägt der ursprüngliche Erzähler die Schuld.
summaries.en: Imam Ali (peace be upon him) instructed that when transmitting a hadith, one must cite the source who narrated it. This teaching was given in the early Islamic period when oral transmission was the primary mode of preserving prophetic knowledge. By attributing the narration to its source, the transmitter is protected: if the hadith is authentic, both gain reward for transmitting truth; if it is fabricated, the blame falls on the original narrator, not the one who faithfully cited his source.
summaries.es: El Imam Ali (la paz sea con él) instruyó que al transmitir un hadiz se debe citar la fuente. Si el hadiz es verdadero, el transmisor obtiene la recompensa; si es falso, el pecado recae sobre el narrador original.
summaries.fa: حضرت علی علیه السلام دستور داده‌اند که هنگام نقل حدیث، آن را به راوی اصلی نسبت دهید. اگر حدیث صحیح باشد ثواب آن برای ناقل است و اگر کذب باشد گناه آن بر عهده راوی اصلی خواهد بود.
summaries.fr: L'Imam Ali (que la paix soit sur lui) a ordonné que lors de la transmission d'un hadith, on doit en citer la source. Si le hadith est authentique, le transmetteur en tire la récompense ; s'il est faux, le péché incombe au narrateur original.
summaries.id: Imam Ali (a.s.) memerintahkan agar ketika meriwayatkan hadits, harus menyebutkan sumbernya. Jika hadits tersebut benar maka perawi mendapat pahala, dan jika palsu maka dosanya ditanggung oleh sumber aslinya.
summaries.ru: Имам Али (мир ему) предписал при передаче хадиса указывать его источник. Если хадис правдив, передающий получает награду; если ложен, грех ложится на первоначального рассказчика.
summaries.tr: Hz. Ali (a.s.) hadis rivayet ederken kaynağın belirtilmesini emretmiştir. Doğru ise rivayet edenin sevabı, yanlış ise asıl nakleden kişinin günahı olur.
summaries.ur: حضرت علی علیہ السلام نے حکم دیا کہ حدیث بیان کرتے وقت اسے اس کے راوی سے منسوب کیا جائے۔ اگر حدیث سچی ہو تو ثواب بیان کرنے والے کو ملے گا اور اگر جھوٹی ہو تو گناہ اصل راوی پر ہوگا۔
summaries.zh: 伊玛目阿里（愿主赐他平安）教导说，传述圣训时必须注明出处。如果圣训是真实的，传述者获得回赐；如果是虚假的，罪过由原始讲述者承担。

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/179)
Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: ضعيف - Sahih al-Kafi

parts.text: عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ

text: قَالَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) إِذَا حَدَّثْتُمْ بِحَدِيثٍ فَأَسْنِدُوهُ إِلَى الَّذِي حَدَّثَكُمْ فَإِنْ كَانَ حَقّاً فَلَكُمْ وَ إِنْ كَانَ كَذِباً فَعَلَيْهِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, and from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Al Nowfaly, from Al Sakuny,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Whenever you narrate a Hadeeth, so link it to the one who narrated it to you. So if it was true, it would be for you, and if it was a lie, so it would be upon him’.
en.sarwar: 7. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid from al-Nawfali from al-Sakuni from abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) who has said the following. “When you would narrate Hadith you must say who its narrator is. If it would be true it will be for you and if it would be false it will be his responsibility.

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/179)
Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: ضعيف - Sahih al-Kafi

parts.text: عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Muhammad Bin Abdullah, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Abu Ayoub Al Madany, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Husayn Al Ahmasy,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The heart relies upon the written (words)’.
en.sarwar: 8. Ali ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Abdallah has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from abu Ayyub al-Madani from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Husayn al-Ahmasi from abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) who has said the following. “The heart relies on writing.”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/180)
Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: ضعيف - Sahih al-Kafi
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ اكْتُبُوا فَإِنَّكُمْ لَا تَحْفَظُونَ حَتَّى تَكْتُبُوا .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Al Washha, from Aasim Bin Humeyd, from Abu Baseer who said,
‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘ You must write down (our Ahadith), for you would not be preserving (Ahadith) until you are writing it down’.
en.sarwar: 9. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from Mu‘alla ibn Muhammad from al-Hassan ibn Ali al-Washsha’ from ‘Asim ibn Humayd from abu Basir who has said the following. “(Imam) abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) said, ‘You must write down because you will not memorize until you write down.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/180)
Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: صحيح - Sahih al-Kafi (1/7)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) احْتَفِظُوا بِكُتُبِكُمْ فَإِنَّكُمْ سَوْفَ تَحْتَاجُونَ إِلَيْهَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Bin Fazzal, from Ibn Bukeyr, from Ubeyd Bin Zurara who said,
‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Be preserving with your books, for very soon you would need those’.
en.sarwar: 10. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from al-Hassan ibn Ali ibn Faddal from ibn Bukayr from ’Ubayd ibn Zurara who has said the following. “Abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) said, ‘You must preserve your books because you will soon need them.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (180/ 1)
Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: ضعيف - Sahih al-Kafi

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ الْبَرْقِيِّ

text: قَالَ لِي أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) اكْتُبْ وَ بُثَّ عِلْمَكَ فِي إِخْوَانِكَ فَإِنْ مِتَّ فَأَوْرِثْ كُتُبَكَ بَنِيكَ فَإِنَّهُ يَأْتِي عَلَى النَّاسِ زَمَانُ هَرْجٍ لَا يَأْنَسُونَ فِيهِ إِلَّا بِكُتُبِهِمْ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid Al Barqy, from one of his companions, from Abu Saeed Al Khaybari, from Al Mufazzal Bin Umar who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said to me: ‘Write and transmit your knowledge among your brethren. So if you were to die, so leave your books as an inheritance, for there would be coming a time of commotion upon the people, they would not be finding comfort (during) in it but except with your books’.
en.sarwar: 11. A number of our people has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid al-Barqi from certain persons of his people from abu Sa‘id al-Khaybari from al-Mufaddal ibn ‘Umar who has said the following. “Abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) said, ‘You must write down (Hadith) and spread your knowledge among your brethren. If you will die your children will inherit your books. A time will come when people will face chaos and they will find no comfort but with their books.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع أو ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (181/ 1)
Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: ضعيف - Sahih al-Kafi
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) إِيَّاكُمْ وَ الْكَذِبَ الْمُفْتَرِعَ قِيلَ لَهُ وَ مَا الْكَذِبُ الْمُفْتَرِعُ قَالَ أَنْ يُحَدِّثَكَ الرَّجُلُ بِالْحَدِيثِ فَتَتْرُكَهُ وَ تَرْوِيَهُ عَنِ الَّذِي حَدَّثَكَ عَنْهُ .

en.hubeali: And by this chain, from Muhammad Bin Ali, raising it, said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Beware of the branched out lie!’ It was said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘And what is the branched out lie?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘A man narrates to you a Hadeeth, so you neglect him and report it from the person from whom he had narrated the Hadeeth.
en.sarwar: 12. It is narrated through the same chain of narrators from Muhammad ibn Ali in a marfu‘ manner from abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) who has said the following. “Abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) said, ‘Beware of the branched-out lies.’ They asked the Imam, “What is branched-out lies?” The Imam replied, “It is when a person narrates a Hadith to you and you would ignore him and narrate it from the person from whom he had narrated the Hadith.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/182)
Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: صحيح - Sahih al-Kafi (1/7)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) أَعْرِبُوا حَدِيثَنَا فَإِنَّا قَوْمٌ فُصَحَاءُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from Jameel Bin Darraj who said,
‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Express our<sup>asws</sup> Ahadeeth in a clear manner; for we<sup>asws</sup> are an eloquent people’.
en.sarwar: 13. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn abu Nasr from Jamil ibn Darraj who has said the following. “Abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) said, ‘Speak our Hadith clearly because we are eloquent people.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/182)
Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: ضعيف - Sahih al-Kafi

parts.text: غَيْرِهِ قَالُوا سَمِعْنَا أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام )

text: حَدِيثِي حَدِيثُ أَبِي وَ حَدِيثُ أَبِي حَدِيثُ جَدِّي وَ حَدِيثُ جَدِّي حَدِيثُ الْحُسَيْنِ وَ حَدِيثُ الْحُسَيْنِ حَدِيثُ الْحَسَنِ وَ حَدِيثُ الْحَسَنِ حَدِيثُ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) وَ حَدِيثُ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ حَدِيثُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ حَدِيثُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ قَوْلُ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Muhammad, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Umar Bin Abdul Aziz, from Hisham Bin Salim and Hammad Bin Usman and someone else who said,

‘We heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘My<sup>asws</sup> Hadeeth is a Hadeeth of my<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup>, and a Hadeeth of my<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup> is a Hadeeth of my<sup>asws</sup> grandfather<sup>asws</sup>, and a Hadeeth of my<sup>asws</sup> grandfather<sup>asws</sup> is a Hadeeth of Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup>, and a Hadeeth of Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> is a Hadeeth of Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, and a Hadeeth of Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> is a Hadeeth of Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup>, and a Hadeeth of Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> is a Hadeeth of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, and a Hadeeth of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> are the Words of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic’.
en.sarwar: 14. Ali ibn Muhammad has narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ‘Umar ibn ’Abd al-‘Aziz from Hisham ibn Salim, Hammed ibn ‘Uthman and others who have said the following. “Abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) said, ‘My Hadith is the Hadith of my father. The Hadith of my father is the Hadith of my grandfather. The Hadith of my grandfather is the Hadith of Imam Husayn. The Hadith of Imam al-Husayn is the Hadith of Imam al- Hassan. The Hadith of Imam al-Hassan is the Hadith of Imam Ali (a.s.). The Hadith of Imam Ali is the Hadith of the holy Prophet (s.a.) and the Hadith of the holy Prophet is the words of Allah, the Majestic, the Glorious.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/182)
Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: ضعيف - Sahih al-Kafi

parts.text: مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ أَبِي خَالِدٍ شَيْنُولَةَ

text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي جَعْفَرٍ الثَّانِي ( عليه السلام ) جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ إِنَّ مَشَايِخَنَا رَوَوْا عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ وَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) وَ كَانَتِ التَّقِيَّةُ شَدِيدَةً فَكَتَمُوا كُتُبَهُمْ وَ لَمْ تُرْوَ عَنْهُمْ فَلَمَّا مَاتُوا صَارَتِ الْكُتُبُ إِلَيْنَا فَقَالَ حَدِّثُوا بِهَا فَإِنَّهَا حَقٌّ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Al Hassan Bin Abu Khalid Shaynoula who said,

‘I said to Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> the 2<sup>nd</sup>, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! Our elders were reporting from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> and Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, and there was intense dissimulation. So they concealed their books and did not report from them. So when they died, the books came to be with us’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Narrate with these, for these are true’.
en.sarwar: 15. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Muhammad ibn al-Hassan ibn abu Khalid Shaynula who has said the following. “I said to abu Ja‘far (a.s.), ‘May Allah take my soul in your service, our shaikhs have narrated Hadith from Imam abu Ja‘far and from Imam abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) and at that time because of fear Taqiyah, concealment was sever. They concealed their books and did narrate from them. When they died their books came to us.’” The Imam said, “You may narrate from them because they contain the truth.”
